name: Sundowners Bar & Grill
description: Legendary beachfront spot in West End with the best sunset views on the island. Fresh seafood and cold Salva Vidas right on the water.
insiderTip: Grab a dock seat by 4:30 PM for golden hour — they fill up fast.
addressDescription: On the main road in West End, right on the water past the dive shops

name: Cannibal Cafe
description: Funky West End hangout with creative dishes and killer cocktails. The fish tacos are legendary among locals and travelers alike.
insiderTip: Try the coconut shrimp — it's the best on the island.
addressDescription: Main drag in West End, look for the colorful sign

name: Vintage Pearl Restaurant
description: West Bay's upscale dining experience with Caribbean fusion cuisine. White tablecloths, ocean views, and a wine list that surprises.
insiderTip: Reserve a table for Friday lobster night — it sells out.
addressDescription: Inside Infinity Bay Resort, West Bay Beach

name: Café Escondido
description: Hidden gem coffee shop in Sandy Bay with the best espresso on the island. Homemade pastries and a quiet garden setting.
insiderTip: The banana bread sells out by 10 AM — come early.
addressDescription: Off the main road in Sandy Bay, behind the church

name: Baleadas Express
description: The island's most authentic baleada spot. Locals line up here for a reason — generous fillings, handmade tortillas, and prices that haven't changed in years.
insiderTip: Order the baleada con todo with extra cheese. Bring cash — no cards.
addressDescription: Near the main market in Coxen Hole

name: Herby's Sports Bar & Grill
description: French Harbour's go-to for burgers, wings, and cold beer. Big screens for game days and a local crowd that keeps things lively.
insiderTip: Thursday is wing night — half price and worth the drive.
addressDescription: Main road through French Harbour, across from the marina

name: The Thai Place
description: Surprisingly authentic Thai food in West End. The owner trained in Bangkok and brings real flavors to the Caribbean.
insiderTip: The green curry with local fish is the move. Ask for it spicy.
addressDescription: Upstairs on the main road in West End

name: Island Fresh Bakery
description: Early morning bakery in West Bay serving fresh bread, croissants, and breakfast sandwiches. Everything baked on-site daily.
insiderTip: Get there before 8 AM for the best selection.
addressDescription: Behind the main beach road in West Bay

name: Hole in the Wall Seafood
description: Oak Ridge's best-kept secret. Fresh catch prepared while you wait in a waterfront shack with the most authentic island experience.
insiderTip: Ask what they caught today and have them grill it whole.
addressDescription: On the waterfront in Oak Ridge, take the water taxi

name: Creole's Rotisserie Chicken
description: Perfectly spiced rotisserie chicken with rice and beans that locals swear by. Fast, flavorful, and insanely affordable.
insiderTip: Half chicken with plantains is the best lunch deal on the island.
addressDescription: Near the Coxen Hole dock, next to the pharmacy

name: Blue Marlin Bar
description: West End's most popular beach bar with toes-in-the-sand seating and rum drinks that hit different at sunset.
insiderTip: Monday night is locals' night — cheaper drinks and a great crowd.
addressDescription: On the beach in West End, near the Half Moon Bay entrance

name: West Bay Lodge Bar
description: Chill beach bar right on West Bay Beach. Simple, unpretentious, and the Salva Vida is always ice cold.
insiderTip: Best value drinks on West Bay Beach — skip the resort bars.
addressDescription: On West Bay Beach, look for the thatched roof

name: Land's End Bar
description: Iconic West End sunset bar perched over the water. The rum punch is famous and the atmosphere is pure island vibes.
insiderTip: Sit on the dock — it's the best sunset seat on Roatán.
addressDescription: At the very end of the main road in West End

name: Splash Inn Pool Bar
description: Swim-up pool bar in West End with a party vibe on weekends. Great cocktails and a fun crowd.
insiderTip: Wednesday is 2-for-1 margaritas — the pool gets packed.
addressDescription: Splash Inn Dive Resort, West End main road

name: Palm Beach Bar
description: Laid-back West Bay beach bar with hammocks, cold beer, and zero pretension.
insiderTip: Order the michelada — they make it with fresh lime.
addressDescription: South end of West Bay Beach

name: Gio's Sports Bar
description: French Harbour's favorite watering hole with pool tables, darts, and a loyal local crowd.
insiderTip: Friday karaoke night is an experience you won't forget.
addressDescription: Main road in French Harbour

name: Coconut Grove Bar
description: Sandy Bay's hidden cocktail spot surrounded by palm trees. Craft cocktails with local ingredients.
insiderTip: Try the passion fruit daiquiri — made with fruit from the garden.
addressDescription: Off the main road in Sandy Bay, follow the signs

name: Pirate's Cove Bar
description: Themed bar in West End with a fun pirate atmosphere and strong pours. Tourist-friendly but the drinks deliver.
insiderTip: The rum bucket is enough for two — don't try to finish it alone.
addressDescription: West End main road, hard to miss the pirate flag

name: Flores Bay Cantina
description: Local spot in Flowers Bay where islanders drink after work. Cold beer, warm people, real Roatán.
insiderTip: Just show up, be friendly, and you'll make friends for life.
addressDescription: Flowers Bay main road, near the dock

name: Coconut Tree Divers
description: Top-rated PADI dive shop in West End with small groups and expert instructors. The best reef diving in the Caribbean starts here.
insiderTip: Book the afternoon 2-tank dive — fewer people and better light.
addressDescription: West End main road, right on the water

name: Reef Gliders
description: West End dive shop known for personalized service and excellent guides. Perfect for beginners and experienced divers.
insiderTip: Ask for Carlos — he knows every turtle on the reef by name.
addressDescription: North end of West End, past the church

name: West Bay Divers
description: Shore diving right off West Bay Beach. Walk in, gear up, and you're on the reef in minutes.
insiderTip: The shore dive here is better than most boat dives elsewhere.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, near the main beach entrance

name: Native Sons Diving
description: Locally owned dive operation in Sandy Bay with deep knowledge of the reef system. Supporting local divers.
insiderTip: They do early morning dives before the crowds — ask about the 7 AM slot.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay waterfront

name: Roatán Snorkel Tours
description: Best snorkeling experience for non-divers. Glass-bottom boat, guided reef tours, and guaranteed turtle sightings.
insiderTip: The afternoon tour has fewer boats and calmer water.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach dock area

name: Submarine Dive Center
description: French Harbour dive shop specializing in south side wall dives and advanced courses.
insiderTip: The south wall dives here are world-class — don't skip them for the tourist sites.
addressDescription: French Harbour marina area

name: Splash Inn Dive Center
description: Full-service dive resort in West End with pool training, daily boats, and all-inclusive dive packages.
insiderTip: The stay-and-dive package saves you hundreds compared to booking separately.
addressDescription: Splash Inn Resort, West End

name: Oak Ridge Dive Adventures
description: Off-the-beaten-path diving on the east end. Pristine reefs with virtually no other divers.
insiderTip: Worth the trip for experienced divers — you'll have the reef to yourself.
addressDescription: Oak Ridge waterfront, near the bridge

name: Seagrape Plantation Snorkel
description: Private snorkel experience in Sandy Bay with a marine biologist guide. Educational and stunning.
insiderTip: Book the private tour for couples — it's unforgettable.
addressDescription: Seagrape Plantation Resort, Sandy Bay

name: Bodden Tours
description: Island-wide tours with genuine local guides who share the real Roatán. Full-day and half-day options.
insiderTip: Ask for Daniel — he was born on the island and knows everyone.
addressDescription: Near the Coxen Hole cruise port

name: South Shore Zipline
description: 8-line zipline course through the jungle canopy with ocean views. Thrilling and safe with professional guides.
insiderTip: Go in the morning when it's cooler — you'll enjoy it more.
addressDescription: South shore road near French Harbour, signs posted

name: Roatán Deep Sea Fishing
description: Full-day and half-day deep sea fishing charters. Marlin, tuna, mahi-mahi — the Mesoamerican reef delivers.
insiderTip: Book the early morning charter for the best chance at marlin.
addressDescription: West End dock area

name: Mangrove Kayak Adventure
description: Guided kayak tour through Roatán's mangrove tunnels. See wildlife up close in a pristine ecosystem.
insiderTip: Bring waterproof sunscreen — you'll be out for 3 hours.
addressDescription: Oak Ridge waterfront, near the gas station

name: Island Glass Bottom Boat
description: See the reef without getting wet. Crystal-clear viewing panels and a knowledgeable captain.
insiderTip: Sit at the front of the boat for the best views.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, near the main dock

name: Barrio Dorcas Horse Ranch
description: Horseback riding on the beach and through tropical trails. Gentle horses suitable for all experience levels.
insiderTip: The sunset ride is magical — book 2 days in advance.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay hills, pickup available

name: Daniel Johnson's Monkey & Sloth Hangout
description: Meet monkeys and sloths up close in a rescue sanctuary setting. Educational, fun, and great for families.
insiderTip: Go right when they open at 8 AM — the animals are most active and it's less crowded.
addressDescription: French Harbour area, well-signed from the main road

name: Punta Gorda Cultural Experience
description: Immersive Garifuna cultural tour including traditional cooking, drumming, and storytelling with community elders.
insiderTip: This is the most authentic cultural experience on Roatán — don't miss it.
addressDescription: Punta Gorda village, ask for the cultural center

name: Bay Islands Helicopter Tours
description: See Roatán from above with a scenic helicopter flight over the reef, jungle, and coastline.
insiderTip: Book the full-island tour — the east end from the air is spectacular.
addressDescription: Near the Roatán airport

name: Yaba Ding Ding Gift Shop
description: West End's quirkiest shop with handmade jewelry, local art, and the best Roatán souvenirs.
insiderTip: The hand-painted coconuts make perfect gifts — each one is unique.
addressDescription: Main road in West End, look for the colorful storefront

name: Roatán Cigar Company
description: Hand-rolled cigars made on the island with Honduran tobacco. Watch them being made and sample the collection.
insiderTip: The maduro is excellent — buy a box, they're a fraction of US prices.
addressDescription: French Harbour main road, next to the marina

name: Rusty Fish Art Gallery
description: Local artist cooperative showcasing paintings, sculptures, and photography inspired by island life.
insiderTip: Ask about commission work — several artists do custom pieces.
addressDescription: West End, second floor above the dive shop

name: Coxen Hole Town Market
description: Bustling local market with produce, spices, household goods, and authentic Honduran products.
insiderTip: Come Saturday morning for the best produce and haggle respectfully.
addressDescription: Town center in Coxen Hole, near the dock

name: Isla Bella Boutique
description: Beach clothing, swimwear, and resort wear at West Bay. Curated collection from Caribbean designers.
insiderTip: End-of-season sales in November have 50% off everything.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach entrance area

name: Garifuna Arts & Crafts
description: Traditional Garifuna handicrafts — woven baskets, drums, dolls, and artwork made by community artisans.
insiderTip: Every purchase supports the Garifuna community directly.
addressDescription: Punta Gorda village main road

name: MegaPlaza Roatán
description: The island's main shopping center with a supermarket, pharmacy, electronics, and everyday essentials.
insiderTip: Stock up on sunscreen and water here — beach shops charge double.
addressDescription: Main highway in Coxen Hole, impossible to miss

name: Reef Treasures Jewelry
description: Custom jewelry featuring sea glass, coral-inspired designs, and sterling silver. Made on the island.
insiderTip: They'll custom-make a pendant from sea glass you find on the beach.
addressDescription: West End, near the end of the main road

name: Infinity Bay Resort
description: West Bay's premier beachfront resort with spacious condos, infinity pool, and direct beach access.
insiderTip: Book a top-floor unit facing the ocean — the sunrise is stunning.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, main resort area

name: Splash Inn Dive Resort
description: West End's best dive resort with pool, restaurant, and in-house dive shop. Great value packages.
insiderTip: The dive-and-stay packages save you hundreds compared to booking separately.
addressDescription: West End main road, right in the heart of town

name: Casa Calico Hostel
description: Budget-friendly hostel in West End with a social vibe, hammocks, and a shared kitchen. Backpacker paradise.
insiderTip: The dorm room upstairs has the best breeze — request it specifically.
addressDescription: West End, behind the main strip

name: Grand Roatán Resort
description: All-inclusive luxury on the west end with private beach, spa, and multiple restaurants.
insiderTip: Ask for a room in building 3 — closest to the beach and quietest.
addressDescription: West Bay, past the main beach area

name: Posada Arco Iris
description: Charming locally-owned guesthouse in West End with colorful rooms and home-cooked breakfast included.
insiderTip: Maria's breakfast is worth the stay alone — ask for the banana pancakes.
addressDescription: Quiet end of West End, near the church

name: Turquoise Bay Dive Resort
description: Eco-lodge on the south side with amazing wall diving right off shore. Remote and beautiful.
insiderTip: The house reef here is the best shore dive on the island.
addressDescription: South shore road, Sandy Bay area

name: Ridge View B&B
description: Cozy bed and breakfast overlooking Oak Ridge harbor. Wake up to the sound of fishing boats.
insiderTip: Ask for the harbor-view room — watch the boats come in at dawn.
addressDescription: Hillside above Oak Ridge harbor

name: Camp Bay Lodge
description: Remote beach lodge at the quiet eastern end of the island. Pure seclusion with the beach to yourself.
insiderTip: Bring everything you need — there are no shops nearby.
addressDescription: End of the road at Camp Bay Beach

name: Mayan Princess Resort
description: Mid-range West Bay resort with full kitchens, pool, and beach access. Great for families.
insiderTip: The 2-bedroom units are perfect for families — book early for holiday weeks.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, south end

name: Captain Van's Scooter Rentals
description: Most popular scooter rental in West End. Well-maintained bikes, helmets included, and fair prices.
insiderTip: Get the full-day rate even if you think you only need half a day — you'll want to explore.
addressDescription: West End main road, near the entrance

name: Roatán Auto Rentals
description: Reliable car rental near the airport. SUVs, sedans, and 4x4s with fair rates and no hidden fees.
insiderTip: Get a 4x4 if you're heading east of French Harbour — the road gets rough.
addressDescription: Near Juan Manuel Gálvez Airport

name: Roatán Kayak Rentals
description: Kayak and paddleboard rentals on West Bay Beach. Single, double, and clear-bottom kayaks available.
insiderTip: The clear-bottom kayak over the reef at sunset is unforgettable.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, near the main entrance

name: E-Bike Island Tours
description: Electric bike rentals for exploring Roatán without breaking a sweat. Self-guided tour maps included.
insiderTip: The battery lasts about 4 hours — plan your route accordingly.
addressDescription: West End main road, near the dive shops

name: West Bay Golf Cart Rentals
description: Cruise around West Bay in style. Golf carts are the local way to get around the beach area.
insiderTip: The 4-seater is perfect for families — fits everyone plus beach gear.
addressDescription: West Bay, near the main road entrance

name: Island Boat Charters
description: Private boat rentals for reef exploring, island hopping, or a day on the water.
insiderTip: The half-day charter to the Cayos Cochinos is the trip of a lifetime.
addressDescription: French Harbour marina

name: Roatán Jet Ski Rentals
description: Jet ski fun on the west end. Guided tours and free-ride options available.
insiderTip: The guided tour takes you to hidden beaches you can't reach by road.
addressDescription: South end of West Bay Beach

name: Reef Ready Snorkel Rentals
description: Quality snorkel equipment rental right on West Bay Beach. Masks, fins, and underwater cameras.
insiderTip: Rent the underwater camera — you'll want photos of the reef.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, main beach area

name: Marvin's Taxi Service
description: Reliable island-wide taxi service. Marvin knows every road and shortcut on Roatán.
insiderTip: Save his WhatsApp — he's the most reliable driver on the island.
addressDescription: Based in Coxen Hole, serves entire island

name: West End Water Taxi
description: Quick water taxi between West End and West Bay. Faster and more scenic than driving.
insiderTip: The last boat back is at 5 PM — don't get stranded.
addressDescription: West End main dock

name: Port Express Shuttle
description: Shuttle service from both cruise ports to West Bay and West End. Air-conditioned vans.
insiderTip: Pre-book online for guaranteed seats on busy cruise ship days.
addressDescription: At both cruise port exits

name: Roatán Airport Transfers
description: Professional airport pickup and drop-off service. Meet-and-greet with name sign.
insiderTip: Book in advance for international flights — taxi availability varies.
addressDescription: Juan Manuel Gálvez Airport

name: Oak Ridge Water Taxi
description: Traditional cayuco water taxis in Oak Ridge. The only way to explore the stilt village.
insiderTip: Agree on the price before you get in — standard rate is about $2-3.
addressDescription: Oak Ridge main dock

name: Roatán Local Bus
description: Public minibus running the main highway from West End to Oak Ridge. Cheap and authentic.
insiderTip: Flag it down anywhere on the main road — there are no official stops.
addressDescription: Main highway, flag down anywhere

name: Bay Island Transfers
description: Private driver service for full-day or half-day island exploration. Custom itineraries.
insiderTip: Book the full-day rate for east end exploration — it's the only comfortable way.
addressDescription: Pickup from any West Bay or West End location

name: Roatán-Utila Ferry
description: Daily ferry service between Roatán and Utila. Catamaran with air conditioning.
insiderTip: Book the morning crossing — afternoon seas can be rough.
addressDescription: Coxen Hole main dock, buy tickets at the terminal

name: West Bay Beach
description: The #1 beach in Honduras and one of the best in the Caribbean. Powdery white sand, crystal water, and reef just offshore.
insiderTip: Walk south past the resorts for a quieter stretch of sand.
addressDescription: West Bay — you can't miss it

name: Half Moon Bay Beach
description: Mellow crescent beach in West End. More chill than West Bay with great snorkeling right off shore.
insiderTip: Best snorkeling is on the north point — look for parrotfish.
addressDescription: North end of West End

name: Camp Bay Beach
description: Remote, pristine, and nearly empty. Worth the drive for those who want a beach entirely to themselves.
insiderTip: Bring everything — water, snacks, sunscreen. There's nothing out there.
addressDescription: End of the road, eastern tip of Roatán

name: Tabyana Beach
description: Private beach resort popular with cruise passengers. Clean facilities, clear water, and organized activities.
insiderTip: Skip the overpriced cruise ship excursion — just take a taxi and pay at the gate.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, south section near Tabyana

name: Sandy Bay Public Beach
description: Quiet local beach in Sandy Bay. Less famous than West Bay but beautiful and far less crowded.
insiderTip: Come on weekdays — you'll often have it to yourself.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay waterfront, follow signs from the main road

name: Punta Gorda Beach
description: Garifuna community beach with warm hospitality. Experience local culture while enjoying the water.
insiderTip: Visit during a Garifuna festival — the drumming and dancing on the beach is magical.
addressDescription: Punta Gorda village waterfront

name: Big Bight Beach
description: Hidden cove near Sandy Bay accessible by a short trail. Calm, clear water perfect for swimming.
insiderTip: The trail starts behind the resort — follow it for 10 minutes.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay, ask locals for the trailhead

name: Port Royal Beach
description: Remote paradise on the far east end. Worth the adventure for pristine sand and world-class snorkeling.
insiderTip: Hire a local boat from Oak Ridge to get there — the road is very rough.
addressDescription: Port Royal, eastern Roatán

name: Sundowners Late Night
description: West End's nightlife epicenter. Live music most nights, dancing, and the party goes until 2 AM.
insiderTip: Thursday is the biggest night — live band and packed dance floor.
addressDescription: West End waterfront, same as daytime Sundowners

name: Eagle Ray's Bar
description: West End party spot with DJs on weekends. The rooftop has the best night views on the strip.
insiderTip: Saturday night is the main event — get there by 10 PM.
addressDescription: West End main road, upstairs with rooftop

name: Beach Bonfire Nights
description: Weekly beach bonfire events on West Bay Beach. Fire poi, live music, and stars over the Caribbean.
insiderTip: Check their Instagram for the next bonfire date — usually Wednesday.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, south end

name: Rooster's Bar
description: Late-night dive bar in West End where locals and travelers mix until the small hours. Cash only.
insiderTip: Don't judge it by the outside — the inside has character.
addressDescription: West End, past the main strip

name: Coco Lounge
description: Upscale cocktail lounge in West Bay. Craft cocktails, ambient music, and a sophisticated crowd.
insiderTip: The espresso martini is the best on the island.
addressDescription: West Bay resort area

name: Harbour Nights Club
description: French Harbour's only nightclub. Local DJs, dancing, and a different vibe from the tourist areas.
insiderTip: Saturday night is the only night worth going — it's dead otherwise.
addressDescription: French Harbour main road, look for the neon sign

name: Paradise Karaoke Nights
description: Karaoke bar in West Bay with a huge song catalog and enthusiastic crowds. Liquid courage sold separately.
insiderTip: Tuesday and Friday are the busiest — come ready to perform.
addressDescription: West Bay, near the resort strip
